title: The Children of Elbaf Panic
description: On pages 13–15, this part follows Unknown person #13, Hajrudin, Unknown person #14, Chopper, Usopp, Unknown person #15 as The Straw Hats explore Elbaf’s strange giant-scale spaces while children, teachers, and old legends point toward the island’s deeper mysteries.
scene: The Straw Hats explore Elbaf’s strange giant-scale spaces while children, teachers, and old legends point toward the island’s deeper mysteries.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: He was already at death's door...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just standing was taking all the strength he could muster.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: It was a nice try.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He probably planned on finding somewhere to pass out safely after he escaped.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I doubt he'd have made it, given the blood loss...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Now it's up to you whether you wanna treat him or let him croak...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I have unfinished business with him!! But he was covered in blood!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It certainly looks like someone tried to kill him, and that's a separate...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: ...issue from his prior crimes.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's just a hunch, but could it be!!!

dialogue.speaker: Hajrudin
dialogue.text: Hajrudin! I have a request!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: PLEASE SAVE HIS LIFE!! I DON'T CARE WHAT KIND OF PERSON HE IS, I CAN'T JUST WATCH HIM DIE!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: How long for a full recovery???

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: several months at the least.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: If we're serious about treating these wounds...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He shouldn't be sleeping in a place like this.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They won't be so forgiving at the village... it'd be a one way ticket to execution!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: I don't mind staying here.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Their hatred really runs deep, but I guess we are talking about unforgivable garbage.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Wow, those horns are the real deal...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It's the mark of the ancient giants,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: Our father Harold was the same.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: You are both my precious sons.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I want you to get along when I'm gone...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and do what you can to support Elbaf.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How can I call such a weak runt...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: My big brother?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You're just a bastard! You'll never be Dad's true heir!!

dialogue.speaker: Hajrudin
dialogue.text: Hajrudin!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #15
dialogue.text: Everyone knows your mom's got dirty blood!!

summary: This part advances the Elbaf Exploration through 30 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
